mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5% over the 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer price inflation erodes real disposable income, which suppresses consumption demand and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, thereby depressing equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: high inflation and restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Positive SPX forward returns occur in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both rise, invalidating the negative return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding aggregate demand, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarterly GDP growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 in a single month while the unemployment rate remains below 4.5%, it confirms a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS exceeds 200000 while UNRATE remains below 4.5%
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive forward returns in more than 40% of instances over a 10-year sample, or forward returns do not significantly outperform the baseline median.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates sector-specific supply chain recovery outpacing residential investment, which boosts industrial corporate earnings and lifts equity prices over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion, rising real GDP growth amplifies corporate leverage effects, driving outsized positive equity returns over the following month as financing costs remain accommodative.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when both GDPC1 and RSAFS expand, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 45% of triggered windows over a 5-year test.
